title: iontrap-reference parameter record
description: Field-level contract for a single quantity in the structured parameter layer. Graph-level invariants (the input/benchmark wall, transitive benchmark inheritance, reference resolution) are NOT expressible here and are enforced by validator/validate.py.

name.description: Unique identifier for the quantity; referenced by other records' derived_from.

value.description: Scalar (symmetric default), vector of numbers (e.g. a k-vector), or a categorical label (e.g. a polarisation geometry or an isotope role). Units are always required, even for dimensionless/categorical values.

units.description: SI unit string. Use 'dimensionless' or '1' for pure numbers; use a category name (e.g. 'role', 'polarisation') for categorical values.

kind.description: The train/test wall. input = the twin consumes it; benchmark = the twin must reproduce it and it must NEVER be fed into parameterisation/fitting.

value.description: Scalar (symmetric) OR {lower, upper} (asymmetric / one-sided). Consumers must not assume symmetry.

ref.description: Key into registries/sources.yaml. Validator enforces resolution.

loc.description: Precise location (eq / table / fig / section), precise enough that another person recovers the number without interpretation.

extraction_note.description: What the quoted number refers to / how it was read (intake interpretation).

subsystem.description: Query axis. Calibration is NOT a subsystem: a calibrated quantity takes the subsystem of the physics it measures.

observation_type.description: How the value was obtained. 'derived' requires non-empty derived_from.

derived_from.description: List of record names this quantity is computed from; [] for directly obtained values.

generation.description: Key into registries/generations.yaml (lineage stage). Validator enforces resolution.

configuration.description: Compositional but shallow: a fixed set of named slots, each a key into registries/configuration_slots.yaml. null only if genuinely apparatus-independent.

measured_on.description: Date the physical quantity was valid (NOT the extraction date). Required for benchmarks (enforced below).

conditions.description: Physical state of validity not captured by the configuration key.

caveats.description: Applicability / breakdown warnings. Distinct from extraction_note (referent) and conditions (state).

allOf.$comment: [conf] a confirmed record must carry a configuration key (may be null only if genuinely apparatus-independent).
